mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy and robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate profitability, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 7.0% for six consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.1%).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a specific threshold, it signals a tightening monetary policy stance by the central bank. This increases the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, potentially slowing economic growth and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS crosses and sustains above 1.0% for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are predominantly positive (average return > 0.5%).

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales combined with strong housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand. This signals a healthy and expanding economy that drives corporate earnings and S&P 500 appreciation over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 5% MoM
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST show sustained strong growth for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, increasing industrial production indicates a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This translates directly to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as investor confidence improves.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent month-over-month growth above 0.2% for six consecutive months during a declared recovery period, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are flat or negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a robust economy with increasing aggregate demand for goods and services. This typically leads to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 performance over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.0% QoQ annualized for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: If GDPC1 consistently reports growth above 2.0% QoQ annualized for two consecutive quarters, yet S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and can prompt the central bank to implement tighter monetary policy. This increases discount rates and negatively impacts S&P 500 valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL shows sustained annualized inflation above 4.0% for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.2%).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market. This supports consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 100K MoM AND UNRATE falls > 0.1pp MoM
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth exceeds 100K and UNRATE falls by more than 0.1 percentage points for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is robust but retail sales growth (RSAFS) is slowing, it suggests an inventory build-up or weakening consumer demand. This imbalance can precede an economic slowdown and negatively impact S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.3% MoM AND RSAFS growth < 0.1% MoM for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows strong growth while RSAFS growth is weak for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.3%).
